title: Blue Iris alert runs a Wink shortcut
desc: 1) Open Blue Iris2) Right-click the camera you want to receive and alert for.3) Choose "Camera Properties"4) Click the "Alerts" tab5) Check the box next to "Request from a web service"6) Click the "Configure" button for "Request from a web service"7) Under "When Triggered" choose "https://"8) enter "maker.ifttt.com/trigger/BlueIris/with/key/"     8a) Replace  (and remove the < & >) with the name of the camera in question"BlueIrisFrontPorch", "BlueIrisBasementDoor", etc...USE THIS AS YOUR EVENT NAME IN THE TRIGGER     8b) Replace  (and remove the < & >) with your unique Maker channel ID"FHY834hTg893kkls24FHt" or whatever.9) Click "OK"s until you are out of camera properties.

title: Notify my family when nobody's at home
desc: Phoning an empty home wastes time and effort. This Applet uses a GroupMe action to notify your family when your home is no longer occupied. Create the trigger by first adding a virtual device named "home is empty" (or whatever you wish) to your HomeSeer system. Then create a HomeSeer event that turns "home is empty" on when motion has not been sensed in the home for some time. When motion is once again sensed, be sure to create a corresponding HomeSeer event to turn "home is empty" off and add a corresponding Applet to notify your family that the home is once again occupied.

title: Use Alexa to control TV volume via Harmony Activity
desc: To make this work, you MUST configure your A/V devices in Harmony to stay on when switching activitiesThis is because Harmony only supports 1 single entertainment activity being active at any given time, and IFTTT only supports Harmony activities as ActionsThen create a new activity using whatever devices you want, but select to leave inputs unchanged (e.g., you want to use the same command to handle volume for blu-ray and for your STB) Name your new activity "volume up" and edit the start sequence to have your A/V sound device's "volume up" button Create a new "volume up" sequence step for each increment you'd like (I use 3) and any delay needed associated with your particular system For the Trigger phrase, I use "volume up", and the associated activity as the Action Set the names however you'd like, and then use the same recipe as the basis for "volume down".N.B This type of configuration can also be used to change channels, just set up an IFTTT recipe and Harmony Activity for each channel you'd like Note that you'll get a lot of clutter of activities on your remote's screen :P.

desc: This "breathes" your LIFX bulbs when an Amazon Echo timer terminates If you are in a part of the house where you might not hear the Echo's timer signal or if you are hearing impaired, this will help you know when your timer is done  You can tweak the colors and number of "breaths" to suit your personal tastes I have the Applet to "breathe" my LIFX bulbs bright green 20 times When you tweak the LIFX action for your needs you can change any of the parameters I have mine set to act on all of the LIFX bulbs in my house.

title: Push Play on Squeezebox Player with Alexa / Amazon Echo

title: Alexa Sample with Maker and Tasker
desc: You can send a message to your phone that Tasker can use to make any number of changesYou'll have to use AutoRemote to make this work, but it's pretty simpleLink the IFTTT to AutoRemoteYou'll get a key to put into Autoremote that will give you an extremely long linkYou have to edit that link (the very end where it says "MESSAGE_HERE")Then, when this task is triggered, it will send an AutoRemote message to your phone with whatever you entered in the "MESSAGE_HERE" place in the link.

title: Wink@Home Alexa Tasker Integration, Run a Shortcut
desc: This is to be used to send commands from Alexa to my Tasker Wink Integration http://goo.gl/YYti3R  *****  See the "Get Wink Commands from IFTTT" item in the "ADDITIONAL PROFILES & TASKS" section for specific instructions *****  The URL field below should be "&message=Wink=:=shortcut activate "  *****  Insert your AutoRemote URL in place of "" Be sure to remove the < & > *****  Replace "" with the name of your shortcut as it appear in the Wink App Make sure you leave "shortcut activate" there, but again, remove the < & >.

title: Check on Grandma
desc: Imagine you bought an Ecobee3 for a senior citizen There are plenty of devices on the market that can detect a fall or injury and allow a person to call for help However, in the unfortunate event a loved one has passed, there are often stories where they do not frequently receive visitors and go unnoticed for days or even weeks.With this Applet, you can receive an email whenever Smart Home/Away is activated on your Ecobee3 If the motion sensor detects no movement in the home for a period of time when they are scheduled to be there, you can be notified via email Elderly who are not mobile often have caregivers come by to help them This would also alert you that a caregiver had not been by to see them on a particular day, and you could call to check that they are OK.

title: Beat the heat!  High temperature alarm!  A must for any Arizona resident!
desc: Do you live in a warm climate, and always wonder if your Air Conditioning is going to go down while you are away?  Have peace of mind with this simple Applet that alerts you if the interior temperature goes above 87 degrees At 87 degrees, operating electronic devices for extended periods of time can cause damage to the device This Applet protects your home, pets, and electronic devices.

title: Automatically Increase Indoor Humidity
desc: Consider purchasing a WeMo switch and humidifier Then, create a recipe where when Ecobee senses an indoor humidity of less than 25%,  a WeMo switch will activate that is connected to a humidifier Add a second trigger to turn the humidifier off after the Ecobee senses a humidity of greater than 30% This will help not just by keeping people from getting dry skin, lips, or waking up with a dry scratchy throat, but also will help keep fine furniture and wood flooring, cabinets, and doors from splitting or warping If you don't have or plan to purchase a WeMo or humidifier, you may use this recipe to have a notification sent to your iOS, Android, or email address each time your indoor humidity is too low with a link to household tips for increasing humidity in your home (http://www.home-wizard.com/how-to-guide/appliances/humidifier/articles-videos/adding-humidity-during-winter.aspx).

title: High Humidity Warning
desc: High humidity detected potential sever system failure or potential flooding - check home for water.

title: Automatically Reduce Indoor Humidity
desc: Consider purchasing a dehumidifier and WeMo switch to automatically activate the dehumidifier when indoor humidity reaches greater than 70%, and a second trigger to turn the dehumidifier off after the Ecobee senses a humidity of less than 65% This will help with problems such as mold, mildew, corrosion, decay, moisture related deterioration, and dust mite infestation.If you don't plan to purchase a dehumidifier and WeMo, you can use this trigger to send you an email reminder each time your humidity gets too high The email will remind you to visit: http://www.criticalcactus.com/reduce-home-humidity/ for tips on lowering your indoor humidity.

title: Save and share your override activity to a Google folder and see what other users are doing, too!
desc: When you override a specified thermostat, anonymous event, event time, temperature, and humidity values are saved to a spreadsheet file in your Google Drive folder that you specifyYou then have the option to share this spreadsheet with the ecobee user community on a public "crowdsourced" Google Drive folder (shortened link is http://bit.ly/1FIU83c)! This is a great way to find out if you run your furnace cooler or warmer than the average user, or if you're just middle of the road!Privacy Note: When adding this Applet, you will need to choose your reporting thermostat, but its Name and Identifier fields WILL NOT be saved to your spreadsheetSpecifically, the ingredients posted to your Google spreadsheet are: ThermostatTime, EventName, EventType, IndoorTemperature, DesiredIndoorCoolTemperature, DesiredIndoorHeatTemperature, IndoorHumidity, and DesiredHumidity.Enjoy sharing and learning!

desc: This checks an Ecobee thermostat to see if indoor temperature is greater than 119 degrees If so, I will receive an IF Notification to alert me of a possible fire Sounds silly, I know, but think it through Many homes that will have an Ecobee system will also have either a security or camera system It is rare for a residential property to also have fire protection A notification that a room has exceeded a very high temperature could indicate a fire and will alert me to switch over to my camera app on my phone and see if I can see a fire from any of the camera locations Those who don't have cameras could phone a neighbor Seconds difference in a fire could mean saving a home or losing it, saving priceless heirlooms or the lives of pets, etc.

title: If my Neurio sensor is offline, send me a text message
desc: There are many reasons a Neurio sensor may be seen as offline by IFTTT There may be a transient service problem in the Neurio system For these, there is nothing more to do than wait for it to be resolved It is possible to get some level of the status by checking for a response from the Neurio Public API status endpoint (https://api.neur.io/v1/status) There are, however, some conditions a user could take action and check on or may wish to have record of Some possibilities that could lead to IFTTT seeing your sensor offline are that the circuit breaker the Neurio sensor is connected to has tripped, the power is out for the home or switched off at the breaker panel, or there is a wi-fi or internet connectivity problem preventing data from being sent to Neurio In the unlikely event you find a hardware fault with your sensor, please email support@neur.io

title: Siri, arm Piper in Stay mode
desc: This recipe will arm your Piper in Stay mode using Siri voice control on iOS devices (iPhone, iPad, Apple Watch, CarPlay) To use this recipe...1 Connect to the IFTTT SMS channel if you haven't previously done so You'll receive your own unique IFTTT SMS phone number with 415 area code.2 In your iOS Contacts app, create a new contact with First Name set to Piper and phone number set to the IFTTT SMS phone number You only need to create this contact once to use this and other Piper IFTTT recipes that use SMS.3 To arm in Stay mode on your iOS device, set Siri to listening mode by holding down the home button on iPhone or 'hey Siri' voice command on Apple Watch.4 Say to Siri, "Tell Piper hashtag stay" Siri will ask to confirm Say yes to send the text message It's important you include hashtag in the message, otherwise it won't work.5 You should receive a confirmation notification your Piper was armed.

title: Siri, arm Piper in Away mode
desc: This recipe will arm your Piper in Away mode using Siri voice control on iOS devices (iPhone, iPad, Apple Watch, CarPlay) To use this recipe...1 Connect to the IFTTT SMS channel if you haven't previously done so You'll receive your own unique IFTTT SMS phone number with 415 area code.2 In your iOS Contacts app, create a new contact with First Name set to Piper and the phone number set to the IFTTT SMS phone number You only need to create this contact once to use this and other Piper IFTTT recipes that use SMS.3 To arm in Stay mode on your iOS device, set Siri to listening mode by holding down the home button on iPhone or 'hey Siri' voice command on Apple Watch.4 Say to Siri, "Tell Piper hashtag away" Siri will ask to confirm Say yes to send the text message It's important you include hashtag in the message, otherwise it won't work.5 You should receive a confirmation notification your Piper was armed.

title: Track daily Fitbit data to a Salesforce Custom Object
desc: - Create a previous custom object. IFTTT only allows to use 5 fields. All of them must be numeric without decimals, except Distance, that must be with 2 decimal places. - Date field should be autopopulated with a formula (a previous day from "createdDate", for example: "CreatedDate - 0.5"). Date field in Salesforce format, doesnt fit with Date in Fitbit format.- You can also may create a formula field in Salesforce with a link to the specific day report in Fitbit web with the format:  www.fitbit.com/{year}/{month}/{day}(The formula could be: 'https://www.fitbit.com/'& TEXT(YEAR(Date__c)) & '/' & TEXT(MONTH(Date__c)) & '/' & TEXT(DAY(Date__c ))'

title: If it's 30 minutes before sunset on weekdays, turn on my WeMo table lamp
desc: This Applet turns on a Belkin WeMo powered table lamp 30 minutes before sunset on weekdays. To create the trigger, first create a HomeSeer virtual device and name it "30 minutes before sunset on weekends" (or whatever you wish). Then, create a HomeSeer event that will turn this virtual device on 30 minutes before sunset. You should also create a corresponding Applet and HomeSeer event to turn the virtual device and WeMo powered table lamp off at the time of your choosing.

title: Synchronize a HomeSeer virtual device with a product (in this case, a WeMo powered table lamp)
desc: This recipe allows HomeSeer to control a WeMo switch module. It can be easily adapted to control any other IFTTT partner device as well. To create the trigger, add a HomeSeer virtual device and name it "WeMo table lamp" (or whatever you wish). Then, use this recipe to turn the WeMo switch off when the HomeSeer virtual device is turned off. Now, create a corresponding recipe to turn the WeMo switch on when the HomeSeer virtual device is turned on. Once this is done, you can add the HomeSeer virtual device to any HomeSeer event you like and these two IFTTT recipes will function as a "bridge" to control the WeMo device.
